page: Microsoft Azure F16amds_v7 is a memory-intensive virtual machine from the Famdsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores on an x86_64 AMD EPYC 9V45 processor operating at 4.5 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. For local storage, the instance is equipped with an 1890 GB local disk and supports Premium Storage. No GPU accelerators are present on this instance type.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates top-tier single-core CPU performance, as evidenced by stress-ng single-core and single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression tests, which all rank in the top 10%. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is strong, placing in the top 25%. However, multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng and overall PassMark CPU scores fall into the average tier. Memory bandwidth tests for both small cached blocks and large uncached blocks also register average performance, alongside average Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput. For lightweight language model workloads, the server achieves top-tier text generation speeds on small 135M models, though performance scales down to average for medium and large models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the F16amds_v7 offers a high density of memory relative to its 16 dedicated vCPUs, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel multi-core scaling. The inclusion of a substantial 1890 GB local disk provides built-in storage capacity, reducing the immediate need for external storage attachments for data-heavy operations. The primary tradeoff lies in its average multi-core and memory bandwidth performance despite its top-tier single-core capabilities. This profile makes the server suitable for memory-intensive databases, single-threaded processing pipelines, caching layers, and lightweight text generation tasks.
description: The Microsoft Azure F16amds_v7 is an x86_64 memory-intensive virtual machine powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. It features 16 dedicated physical cores, 128.0 GB of RAM (8.0 GB per core), and an 1890 GB local disk with Premium Storage capabilities. In benchmark testing, the instance delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression speeds, alongside strong multi-threaded decompression. However, its multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations fall within the average tier. Lacking a GPU, it relies on its high memory-to-vCPU ratio and built-in local storage to handle data-heavy workloads. This configuration is suited for memory-intensive databases, caching layers, single-threaded data processing, and lightweight language model inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F16amds_v7 is a memory-intensive VM featuring 16 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 128 GB RAM, and an 1890 GB local disk, delivering top-tier single-core CPU performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F16amds_v7 is a memory-intensive VM with 16 AMD EPYC cores, 128 GB RAM, and an 1890 GB local disk for database and caching workloads.
tagline: A memory-intensive virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC cores, substantial local storage, and top-tier single-core processing performance.

128.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
1890 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
